entry.title: Washington Post: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Ukrainian forces pushed from Severodonetsk city center; NATO chief to Sweden
  • Philippines dispatch: Pigs, presstitutes and how journalists are harassed
  • Costly, scandal-ridden mass testing is China’s latest ‘zero covid’ bet
  • Afghans go hungry as U.S. and Taliban officials blame each other
  • North Korea fires artillery shells after arms pledge, South Korea says
  • Russia likely to seize control of eastern Ukrainian region within weeks, U.S. official says
  • Putin makes his imperial pretensions clear
  • De-arched former Moscow McDonald’s reopens without Big Macs
  • How Shireen Abu Akleh was killed
  • Iraqi leaders vow to move ahead after dozens quit parliament
  • UK reports 104 more cases of monkeypox, mostly in men
  • Report finds 196 clerics abused minors in German diocese
  • Pope nixes procession but plans Mass for Congolese on July 3
  • UN, elder: Week of tribal clashes in Sudan’s Darfur kill 100
  • 2 members of Iran's Revolutionary Guard die amid tensions
  • European 'star survey' reveals celestial treasure trove
  • UK's deportation plan attacks 'basic dignity,' lawyers say
  • Bachelet won't seek 2nd term as UN human rights chief
  • Spain: 22 injured as engine collides with passenger train
  • Assistant police chief gets $1.5 million payout after hanging Nazi symbol
  • A Minneapolis woman was about to be evicted. Neighbors bought her home for her.
  • Newspaper apologizes to Rebel Wilson over plans to out her relationship
  • Court filing offers new evidence of post-election breach in Coffee County, Ga.
  • Jan. 6 hearing live updates: Trump 2020 campaign manager pulls out of testifying live
  • Panel set to examine origin of Trump’s ‘big lie’ in second hearing
  • Inside Biden’s frustration with soaring prices
  • Next Jan. 6 hearings to focus on how Trump’s ‘big lie’ fueled rioters
  • S&P 500 slides 3 percent amid global sell-off to tip into bear market
  • Microsoft enters agreement to respect Activision Blizzard unionization
  • Amazon calls cops, fires workers in attempts to stop unionization nationwide
  • You agreed to what? Doctor check-in software harvests your health data.
  • Looking to rent your home? Here’s what to consider.
  • IRS rules about capital gains may lead to questions. We have answers.
  • Historic, four-bedroom house in Charles County lists at $355,000
  • At Last, a Bipartisan Effort on Gun Safety
  • The Scam of Authenticity
  • To Fill the Job Gap, Rehiring Older Could Be Wiser
  • Threat to Kavanaugh Will Irrevocably Change Justices’ Lives
  • How Biden Wants to Trim a Mountain of Student Debt
  • Waive Pharma’s Vaccine Rights? What That Would Mean
  • How the ‘Energy Shock’ Has Upended the Global Economy
  • Behind the Nickel Mess on the London Metal Exchange
  • We can’t let Ukraine lose. It needs a lot more aid, starting with artillery.
  • The GOP is wrong. It’s not that hard to break with Trump.
  • Why today’s Jan. 6 revelations will be dangerous for Trump
  • Cartoon by Pia Guerra
  • How will gun violence affect the Virginia midterms?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Fox News plans to show Jan. 6 hearing after skipping opening night.
  • What to Know About Bear Markets
  • India’s Economy Is Growing Quickly. Why Can’t It Produce Enough Jobs?
  • Gun Deal Is Less Than Democrats Wanted, but More Than They Expected

  • The Celtics Got Lucky by Not Getting What They Wanted
  • It’s Not Easy Being President

  • She Wrote a Dystopian Novel. What Happened Next Was Pretty Dystopian.

  • Just 5 Months Into His Term, Adams Is Busy Raising Money to Win Another

  • French Elections: Pro-Macron Forces Expected to Prevail After First Round
  • Coming From Separate Worlds in Taiwan, They Collided at California Church
  • Sonia Gandhi, India’s opposition leader, has been hospitalized with ‘Covid-related issues’.
  • ‘We Buried Him and Kept Walking’: Children Die as Somalis Flee Hunger
  • Alexander Nikitin, Who Coached a Chess Champion, Dies at 87
  • A Caravan of Migrants Is Heading Toward the U.S. Border

  • Tough Questions for West as Ukraine Cities Teeter

  • China Won’t Hesitate to Fight for Taiwan, Defense Minister Warns

  • Recording India’s Linguistic Riches as Leaders Push Hindi as Nation’s Tongue
  • Russia Opens Rebranded McDonald’s Restaurants, Without the Golden Arches
  • Single Cigarettes in Canada Will Be Inscribed With Warning
  • ‘A Start’: Gun Deal Stirs Hope but Also Frustration in Places Scarred by Shootings
  • Dozens of White Supremacists Arrested in Idaho Had Planned to Riot, Authorities Say
  • U.S. Navy Plans ‘Safety Pause’ After Series of Aircraft Crashes
  • Why Bill Stepien Pulled Out of Monday’s Jan. 6 Hearing
  • California to Launch 988, a New Mental Health Hotline Like 911
  • Two Targets of Trump’s Ire Take Different Paths in South Carolina
  • Pfizer Vaccine Effective in Children Under 5, the F.D.A. Says
  • Jan. 6 Hearing Delayed After Key Witness Cancels In-Person Appearance.
  • Heat Wave Persists in Southwest as High Temperatures Set Records
  • Senators Reach Bipartisan Deal on Gun Safety
  • Sarah Palin Leads Primary Race for Alaska’s Special Election
  • Pence Aide Warned Against Blocking Electoral College Count, Memo Shows

  • Goldman Sachs Is Being Investigated Over E.S.G. Funds
  • Microsoft Pledges Neutrality in Union Campaigns at Activision
  • Disney loses bid for the rights to stream Indian Premier League cricket matches.
  • Preet Bharara’s New Gig
  • New Experimental Therapy for A.L.S. Approved in Canada
  • Striking truck drivers in South Korea snarl supply chains.
  • Global Stocks Plunge on Inflation and Growth Fears

entry.title: Guardian: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v New Zealand: second Test, day four – live!
  • Jan 6 hearings: second hearing begins as Capitol attack committee builds case against Trump – live
  • UN human rights chief says no to second term after China trip
  • Phil Bennett remembered as a ‘natural talent’ and ‘legend of a player’ for Wales
  • Boris Johnson says NI protocol bill a ‘trivial set of adjustments’ as over half of assembly members reject it in ‘strongest terms’– live
  • Julee Cruise obituary
  • Two bodies found in search for Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira, Brazilian embassy says
  • Russia-Ukraine war: Moscow using ‘weapons of mass destruction’, says Finland; 1,200 bodies remain unidentified, say police – live
  • Spiderhead review – Netflix’s prison-experiment fable is going nowhere
  • ‘I hadn’t slept for 10 days’ – the Lemonheads on making It’s a Shame About Ray
  • Amber Heard calls out ‘unfair’ role of social media in Johnny Depp case

  • Patrice Evra: ‘I don’t want kids to live the way I lived for so many years’
  • Spain and southern France hit by second extreme heat event of year
  • Eagles wide receiver Devon Allen runs third-fastest 110m hurdles in history
  • Two shows dominated the Tony awards and proved Broadway’s debt to British taxpayers
  • Thomasina Miers’ recipe for chicken cacciatore | The new flexitarian
  • Arron Banks loses libel action against reporter Carole Cadwalladr
  • UK ramps up gas and oil exports to EU amid Russia’s war in Ukraine
  • The big idea: why we shouldn’t be levelling up
  • Rory McIlroy aims dig at Greg Norman after retaining Canadian Open title
  • Crypto lender Celsius halts withdrawals due to ‘extreme market conditions’
  • Patriotism, optimism and striding ahead: the often overlooked story of Muslim Britain | Miqdaad Versi
  • ‘You’re either called a snowflake or a boomer’: the football coach bringing generations together
  • Emmanuel Macron’s coalition level with new leftwing group in French elections
  • Prince Andrew to miss Windsor Castle procession after ‘family decision’
  • First Rwanda deportation flight could be cancelled amid legal battles
  • Fears grow for Sievierodonetsk civilians as plant struck and bridge blown up
  • Allergies in overdrive as extreme weather drives higher pollen count
  • Mark of prestige: exhibition to explore culture and history of tattoos
  • How we met: ‘I noticed she was transgender, and remember thinking she seemed sweet and shy’
  • Rachel Roddy’s recipes for easy summer pasta | A kitchen in Rome
  • Darwin Núñez: a rampaging bull who should be an ideal fit for Liverpool | Andy Brassell
  • Can’t face another sad salad? Here’s how to ditch the lettuce and forage for something more exciting
  • Weather tracker: heatwaves could add to energy problems across Europe
  • Ozzy Osbourne to undergo major surgery ‘to determine rest of his life’
  • ‘How can I not get enraged?’ Russian director Serebrennikov on war, exile and his new opera
  • ‘Ferocious’ Covid outbreak in Beijing traced to raucous bar
  • Gaia probe reveals stellar DNA and unexpected ‘starquakes’
  • Don’t be complacent, another Covid wave is coming. Here’s how we can manage it | Devi Sridhar
  • Cameron Norrie is a British tennis success story sneaking under the radar | Hannah Jane Parkinson
  • Ukraine helps feed the world – but its farmers, seeds and future are in danger | Michael Fakhri and Sofia Monsalve
  • A local’s guide to Hossegor, France: sand, surf and the best sunsets in the world
  • Best running gear: top gadgets to keep you motivated
  • A new start after 60: ‘Selling vintage clothes takes me back to happier days’
  • Growing numbers of young Africans want to move abroad, survey suggests
  • Lolita at 60: Stanley Kubrick’s daring drama is a deft tightrope act
  • ‘I put on 40 pounds of muscle. Holy mackerel!’ Pablo Schreiber on playing Halo’s ripped hero
  • Millions of men support our abortion rights. We need to help them become stronger allies | Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
  • Russia-Ukraine war: what we know on day 110 of the invasion
  • More than 15,000 sheep drown after live export ship sinks in Sudan
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 13 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-13 14:39PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong was not British colony as China did not recognise unequal treaties ceding city to Britain, new textbooks reveal
  • Scammers con 16 Hongkongers out of HK$4.29 million in recent months, use stolen personal information to apply for fraudulent loans
  • October 12, 2016: ‘Declaration of war as Hong Kong Legco opens’
  • Over a third of Hong Kong tech workers expect a pay rise of at least 10pc but city’s employers only willing to give 3pc: survey
  • June 18, 2015: Hong Kong government’s reform plan fails, as does Legco walkout
  • Hong Kong’s privacy watchdog takes down 90 per cent of social media posts deemed to constitute doxxing under new law
  • June 12, 2013: US spy net targets Hong Kong, Edward Snowden reveals to SCMP
  • Consumption vouchers: Hong Kong scheme extended to give non-PR residents, foreign students and ‘talents’ HK$5,000 handout
  • September 28, 2014: Hong Kong police fire tear gas as thousands join Occupy Central
  • Hong Kong police search for girls after video showing 12-year-old slapped 20 times in the face goes viral
  • Company director, 2 jobless men remanded in custody in connection with gang skirmish in Hong Kong nightlife district
  • October 27, 2012: Non-locals in Hong Kong hit with new property tax
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong schools report highest number of infections since return to classrooms
  • September 12, 2005: Dazzling start to Hong Kong Disneyland carnival
  • April 1, 2003: Pop star Leslie Cheung dies in fall from Hong Kong hotel
  • 2 workers on gondola plunge about 20 floors to their deaths at Hong Kong residential block
  • August 22, 1999: China Airlines plane crashes at Hong Kong airport while landing amid typhoon
  • July 6, 1998: ‘Roger, over and out, Kai Tak’ - Hong Kong’s old airport closes
  • Is Hong Kong a great place to live? How quality of life changed post-handover
  • Departing Hong Kong property investors haunted by same dilemma of 1997, where will the best real estate returns be found?
  • Data on asylum seekers detained in Hong Kong should be disclosed more proactively, legal expert says
  • More than 60 per cent of coronavirus-related deaths in Hong Kong were ‘likely caused by Covid-19’, figure higher among older residents
  • Global capital flows and geopolitical risks are Hong Kong’s biggest challenges for the next few years, city’s de facto central banker says
  • Chinese exporters stay resilient amid increased competition from Vietnam, other emerging markets, Alibaba executive says
  • China GDP: nearly 11 billion Covid tests seen giving economy a US$26 billion boost in second quarter
  • China’s struggling private firms tasked with lifting nation of out economic doldrums
  • China has major vaccine role to play in future health challenges: Gavi
  • China insists it has sovereign rights over Taiwan Strait
  • China and Pakistan, jolted by Karachi attack that killed Chinese, vow stronger military and security ties
  • ‘Resolutely resist violence’: UK fashion label Boy London denounces brutality after publicly linked with gang who viciously attacked women in north China
  • China’s largest academic paper database CNKI opens some services to individuals after Beijing’s antitrust probe
  • China and Australia break diplomatic drought with ‘frank’ defence ministers talks
  • Beijing’s ban on Taiwanese grouper imports prompts emergency measures to help island’s fish farmers
  • Chinese capital tightens Covid controls to stamp out spread of bar cluster
  • Huawei’s cloud service down for half an hour, disrupting services for Guangdong customers
  • What’s fuelling China’s lithium rush in Zimbabwe? The long game on zero carbon
  • Say it with aircraft carriers: why countries send in the big ships
  • Don’t turn South Pacific into US-China geopolitical ‘boxing ring’, Beijing says
  • China is wooing Pacific nations – but can it win trust?
  • UN human rights chief Bachelet says no to second term, ‘no relationship’ to China trip
  • North Korea ‘has 20 nuclear warheads at most’, security think tank says
  • Japan’s health conscious turn to ‘swamp’ food in bid to lose weight but feel full
  • Is Japan’s reopening to tourists triggering a series of ‘kamikaze car’ attacks between ‘yakuza’ gangs?
  • Japan passes bill to make online insults punishable by jail time, following suicide of Hana Kimura, star of Netflix show ‘Terrace House’
  • Singapore’s Lawrence Wong serves as acting PM as Lee Hsien Loong takes a week-long break
  • Ukraine war: India, China and other Asian nations growing markets for shunned Russian oil
  • Malaysia losing billions in sales as firms turn down orders due to migrant labour shortage
  • South Korea economy faces risks as trucker strike enters 7th day
  • Netflix confirms Squid Game will return for a second season
  • India police charge 30 soldiers for killing six tribal labourers
  • Indian officials step up arrests, demolish houses to stop unrest over anti-Islam remarks
  • Optimism in Indonesia as youth prospects improve amid economic recovery
  • Coronavirus: Travel firms in state of confusion as Japan reopens to foreign tourists
  • India’s Sonia Gandhi, opposition leader, hospitalised with Covid-related issues
  • North Korea fires suspected artillery pieces into sea, says Seoul military
  • No vaccine, no can do: Rudy Giuliani’s son Andrew must join NY political debate virtually, because he’s not been jabbed
  • UK to defy EU by scrapping Northern Ireland trade rules: ‘bureaucratic simplifications’, says Prime Minister Johnson
  • Russia’s ex-PM says Vladimir Putin ‘out of it’, Ukraine war could last two years
  • Google suspends engineer who publicly claimed he interacted with a sentient AI bot
  • One way out of Ukraine’s Sievierodonetsk after Russia destroys bridge
  • Google pays US$118 million to settle gender discrimination lawsuit that says it underpaid women
  • Teen used small drone to help Ukraine’s forces target Russian tanks and trucks
  • Amnesty accuses Russia of war crimes in Ukraine’s Kharkiv, where civilians died in ‘indiscriminate’ attacks
  • Brazil police, indigenous search teams, deny bodies found of missing men Dom Phillips and Bruno Pereira
  • John Allen resigns as Brookings president amid FBI foreign lobbying probe
  • World headed for new era of nuclear rearmament, SIPRI says
  • January 6 panellists: enough evidence to indict Donald Trump
  • Emmanuel Macron’s majority in doubt after first round of French parliament vote
  • Tajikistan security services kill two ‘criminal leaders’ in restive east
  • Five teens shot in Kentucky after anti-gun rally
  • ‘No Rwanda’ protest held at UK immigration removal centre
  • US senators reach limited deal addressing gun violence after mass shootings
  • Dozens still missing two months after South Africa’s worst floods killed hundreds
  • Ukraine war: Pro-Russian separatists uphold foreigners’ death sentences
  • Chinese cryptocurrency-mining rig maker Nano Labs eyes US$50 million Nasdaq IPO defying clampdown at home
  • Goldman sees end of selling in Chinese stocks, but CCB, Everbright say only volatility is assured
  • Recession fears are growing, but the global growth outlook isn’t all bad
  • Hong Kong stocks slide by most in 5 weeks as Alibaba, tech peers sink on China lockdown concerns and Fed tightening path
  • Electric Last Mile plans to liquidate, becoming the first SPAC-tied EV start-ups to go bust
  • Microsoft’s new Xbox Game Pass slate boosts ‘Netflix of gaming’ with blockbuster titles
  • Future of work: How can businesses Achieve Positive-Sum Growth using AI-Driven Solutions
  • China’s US$1.3 trillion stock rebound rewards contrarian funds with three barriers challenging path to next level
  • Building an entrepreneurial mindset in the young to harness the potential of the Greater Bay Area
  • 5 fashion collections that celebrate Pride Month and the LGBT community, from Tom Daley sweater NFT to Cher T-shirt with Versace
  • From high cholesterol, high blood pressure and prediabetes to healthiest he’s ever been – how an ex-CEO turned his health around
  • Chinese Nezha legend inspires Hong Kong Dance Company production that’s inventive and inspiring
  • Luxury dog kennel design competition draws Foster + Partners architects, former Apple design chief and bespoke furniture maker David Linley among others
  • A dream come true for Gin Lee: pop, jazz and tango battle it out in a Le French May concert in Hong Kong unlike anything she has done before
  • Will Cyprus’ City of Dreams Mediterranean casino resort be a safe bet, with no Russian gamblers and fears of money laundering?
  • Procter & Gamble Hong Kong and Mannings take Japanese premium hair care brand Hair Recipe to new heights
  • What Vietnam’s first luxury train carriage is like to ride – a soothing, at times surreal, journey between Da Nang and Quy Nhon
  • Is Hong Kong cinema dead in an era of mainland China co-productions? No, an exciting new generation of local filmmakers says
  • Domestic abuse in ancient China was common and a woman’s punishment for divorcing her husband was harsh, but one woman said ‘enough’
  • Where has Star Wars’ Hayden Christensen been? He’s back as Anakin Skywalker in Disney+’s Obi-Wan Kenobi, but after early fame – and dating Natalie Portman – he traded Hollywood for life on the farm
  • The 8 best steak and grill restaurants in Hong Kong, from the Grand Hyatt Steakhouse and Ocean Park Marriott Hotel’s Prohibition Grill House & Cocktail Bar, to Rosewood Hong Kong’s Henry
  • 5 scandalous K-pop idols who fans urged to leave their groups: Lesserafim’s Garam, NCT’s Lucas, Exo’s Chen and Chanyeol, and Red Velvet’s Irene all went on hiatus
  • The problem with fashion NFTs? You can’t wear them everywhere – Nike, Burberry and Dolce & Gabbana may be cashing in on digital wearables, but users need to pick their metaverse
  • Elon Musk vs Bill Gates – what are they fighting about now? The billionaire Microsoft and Tesla founders’ Twitter beef over Covid-19, bitcoin, electric cars, space travel, climate change ... and more
  • Inside Tom Cruise’s multimillion-dollar collection of cars, motorbikes and planes: the Top Gun: Maverick star owns luxury BMWs from Mission: Impossible, rare Porsches and even a vintage WWII jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Financial Times: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Wall Street stocks open in bear market as sell-off accelerates
  • US set for recession next year, economists predict
  • UK economy unexpectedly shrinks as prices surge
  • The WTO’s lonely struggle to defend global trade
  • Divisions in the west threaten Ukraine
  • Google places engineer on leave after he claims group’s chatbot is ‘sentient’

  • Teen curfews fail to keep youngsters safe

  • An insider guide to a green weekend in London
  • America must do more in the trade tug of war
  • John Allen resigns from Brookings over Qatar lobbying allegations
  • India’s tech sector switches on to renewable electricity
  • Sky backs £100mn climate investment fund
  • Google agrees $118mn settlement over claim it underpaid women
  • The best new cameras for summer 2022
  • Celsius melts as ether smoked
  • BP sells stake in Canadian oil sands to push into offshore projects
  • Killing coal: a new way to get investors involved
  • De Nora sticks to IPO plan as hydrogen rush trumps market chaos
  • Business Education: Financial Training
  • RIP ESG?
  • Investor LGIM airs doubts over £1.4bn tie-up of Capricorn Energy and Tullow Oil
  • Passive investing has increased US stock volatility, study finds
  • Investor flight from China reverses as Beijing shifts sentiment
  • UK regulator puts Credit Suisse on watchlist after scandals
  • Fintechs seek graduates who can straddle specialisms
  • Asia-Pacific Climate Leaders
  • China ramps up coal power to boost post-lockdown growth
  • Bank Australia sees climate action as key to customer growth

  • European pension funds attack Toyota for not going ‘all in’ on electric cars

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
